---
title: "Dichanthelium webberianum"
slug: "dichanthelium-webberianum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180846807/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Dichanthelium webberianum"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180846807/medium.jpeg" alt="Dichanthelium webberianum" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/deanna_d">
deanna_d</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107518297">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=1009955)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Rare to Florida found in moist pine savannahs and flatwoods.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its slender stems, narrow leaves, and small, delicate seed heads typical of panicgrasses. In Florida, it is found in sandy, open habitats such as scrub, flatwoods, and pine barrens. It grows primarily during the warmer months and contributes to ground cover in native plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/1009955-Dichanthelium-webberianum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3080)
